methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

data.narrative:
NextSilicon, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Elad Raz, has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis incorporates 5 same-sector, same-stage private peers (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, Photoroom) and 1 public comparable (PLTR), establishing a relevant peer universe for triangulation.

Two methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $200M total raised as a valuation proxy, implying $380M. Peer-median methodology reflects the median valuation of five comparable Series B AI & Software companies at $5.0B, with the peer set spanning from $500M (Photoroom) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence). This wide dispersion reflects genuine heterogeneity in frontier-tech valuations at this stage, driven by differences in capital intensity, revenue traction, and perceived technical defensibility.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $323M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.7B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around NextSilicon's position within its peer cohort. Key sensitivities include: the degree to which the company has demonstrated differentiated technical capability or revenue traction relative to peers; the capital efficiency of its business model; and broader market appetite for AI infrastructure versus application-layer risk. Additional diligence on unit economics and technical moat would narrow this range materially.
